title: Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning
summary: DARP is a retrieval-based imitation learning approach that improves policy performance by retrieving nearest-neighbor demonstrations and computing difference vectors between query and retrieved neighbor states. These difference vectors are processed through a neural network and aggregated with retrieved action predictions to produce the final policy output. The method achieves a 2.1x improvement in real robot success rate on FurnitureBench (44.0% to 92.0%) and 1.5x–5.2x gains in simulation across 10+ tasks, and is compatible with diffusion policies.
